summary: Penalises anyone who, with knowledge or reason to believe, harbours a deserter from the armed forces, except as specified, with up to two years' imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine or with both
ingredients: knowing or having reason to believe that an officer, soldier, sailor or airman has deserted
harbours such deserter
exceptions: Exception . — This provision does not extend to the case in which the harbour is given by the spouse of the deserter.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 164, Page 50

description: The master or person in charge of a merchant vessel, on board of which any deserter from the Army, Navy or Air Force of the Government of India is concealed, shall, though ignorant of such concealment, be liable to a penalty not exceeding three thousand rupees, if he might have known of such concealment but for some neglect of his duty as such master or person in charge, or but for some want of discipline on board of the vessel.
summary: Imposes a monetary penalty of up to Rs 3,000 on a ship's master or person in charge who could have known about a concealed military deserter but failed to due to neglect or lack of discipline.
punishment: penalty not exceeding three thousand rupees
ingredients: master or person in charge of a merchant vessel where a deserter is concealed
could have known of the concealment but for neglect of duty or lack of discipline
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 165, Page 50

description: Whoever abets what he knows to be an act of insubordination by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman, in the Army, Navy or Air Force, of the Government of India, shall, if such act of insubordination be committed in consequence of that abetment, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
summary: Criminalises abetment of a known act of insubordination by military personnel when the insubordination occurs because of the abetment, punishable by up to two years' imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: abetting a known act of insubordination by an officer, soldier, sailor or airman
the act of insubordination is committed as a consequence of the abetment
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 166, Page 50

description: No person subject to the Air Force Act, 1950, the Army Act, 1950 and the Navy Act, 1957, or shall be subject to punishment under this Sanhita for any of the offences defined in this Chapter.
summary: Provides that persons already covered by the Air Force, Army, or Navy Acts are not punishable under this Sanhita for offences defined in the same chapter.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 167, Page 50

description: Whoever, not being a soldier, sailor or airman in the Army, Naval or Air service of the Government of India, wears any garb or carries any token resembling any garb or token used by such a soldier, sailor or airman with the intention that it may be believed that he is such soldier, sailor or airman, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to two thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: Penalises civilians who wear or carry military-style garb or tokens with the intent to be mistaken for a soldier, sailor or airman, with up to three months' imprisonment or a fine up to Rs 2,000.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to two thousand rupees, or with both
ingredients: person is not a soldier, sailor or airman
wears garb or carries token resembling that of such personnel
intention that it may be believed he is such soldier, sailor or airman
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 168, Page 50

description: For the purposes of this Chapter— ( a ) “candidate” means a person who has been nominated as a candidate at any election; ( b ) “electoral right” means the right of a person to stand, or not to stand as, or to withdraw from being, a candidate or to vote or refrain from voting at an election.
summary: Provides definitions for “candidate” and “electoral right” used in the election offences chapter.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 169, Page 50

description: ( 1 ) Whoever— ( i ) gives a gratification to any person with the object of inducing him or any other person to exercise any electoral right or of rewarding any person for having exercised any such right; or Abetment of such assault, if assault committed. Abetment of desertion of soldier, sailor or airman. Harbouring deserter. Deserter concealed on board merchant vessel through negligence of master. Abetment of act of insubordination by soldier, sailor or airman. Persons subject to certain Acts. Wearing garb or carrying token used by soldier, sailor or airman. Candidate, electoral right defined. Bribery. 45 of 1950. 46 of 1950. 62 of 1957. ( ii ) accepts either for himself or for any other person any gratification as a reward for exercising any such right or for inducing or attempting to induce any other person to exercise any such right, commits the offence of bribery: Provided that a declaration of public policy or a promise of public action shall not be offence under this section. ( 2 ) A person who offers, or agrees to give, or offers or attempts to procure, a gratification shall be deemed to give a gratification. ( 3 ) A person who obtains or agrees to accept or attempts to obtain a gratification shall be deemed to accept a gratification, and a person who accepts a gratification as a motive for doing what he does not intend to do, or as a reward for doing what he has not done, shall be deemed to have accepted the gratification as a reward.
summary: Defines the offence of bribery in elections: giving or accepting gratification to induce or reward the exercise of any electoral right, with provisions on what does not constitute an offence and how offers and acceptances are deemed.
ingredients: gives a gratification to any person with the object of inducing or rewarding the exercise of any electoral right
accepts any gratification as a reward for exercising or inducing the exercise of any electoral right
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 170, Page 50

description: ( 1 ) Whoever voluntarily interferes or attempts to interfere with the free exercise of any electoral right commits the offence of undue influence at an election. ( 2 ) Without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of sub-section ( 1 ), whoever— ( a ) threatens any candidate or voter, or any person in whom a candidate or voter is interested, with injury of any kind; or ( b ) induces or attempts to induce a candidate or voter to believe that he or any person in whom he is interested will become or will be rendered an object of Divine displeasure or of spiritual censure, shall be deemed to interfere with the free exercise of the electoral right of such candidate or voter, within the meaning of sub-section ( 1 ). ( 3 ) A declaration of public policy or a promise of public action or the mere exercise or a legal right without intent to interfere with an electoral right, shall not be deemed to be interference within the meaning of this section.
summary: Creates the offence of undue influence at elections, covering voluntary interference, threats, or spiritual intimidation that impedes the free exercise of electoral rights, while excluding mere statements of public policy.
ingredients: voluntarily interferes or attempts to interfere with the free exercise of any electoral right
threatens a candidate or voter with injury
induces a candidate or voter to believe they will incur divine displeasure or spiritual censure
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 171, Page 51

description: Whoever at an election applies for a voting paper on votes in the name of any other person, whether living or dead, or in a fictitious name, or who having voted once at such election applies at the same election for a voting paper in his own name, and whoever abets, procures or attempts to procure the voting by any person in any such way, commits the offence of personation at an election: Provided that nothing in this section shall apply to a person who has been authorised to vote as proxy for an elector under any law for the time being in force in so far as he votes as a proxy for such elector.
summary: Criminalises personation at elections by applying for a ballot in another's name (real, dead, or fictitious) or re‑applying in one's own name after voting, and also covers abetment, with a proxy exception.
ingredients: applies for a voting paper in the name of any other person (living, dead, or fictitious) or in own name after having already voted
abets, procures or attempts to procure voting by any person in such manner
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 172, Page 51

description: Whoever commits the offence of bribery shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which m
summary: Prescribes punishment for the offence of bribery, though the text is incomplete in this excerpt.
ingredients: commits the offence of bribery
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 173, Page 51

title: Punishment for undue influence or personation at an election
description: Whoever commits the offence of undue influence or personation at an election shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year or with fine, or with both.
summary: Any person who commits undue influence or personation during an election is liable to imprisonment up to one year, a fine, or both.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year or with fine, or with both
ingredients: commits the offence of undue influence or personation at an election
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 174, Page 51

title: False statement in connection with an election
description: Whoever with intent to affect the result of an election makes or publishes any statement purporting to be a statement of fact which is false and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, in relation to the personal character or conduct of any candidate shall be punished with fine.
summary: Making or publishing a false statement about a candidate’s character or conduct, with intent to influence election results, is punishable by fine.
punishment: fine
ingredients: makes or publishes a false statement purporting to be a statement of fact
knows or believes the statement to be false or does not believe it to be true
statement relates to personal character or conduct of any candidate
intent to affect the result of an election
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 175, Page 51

title: Illegal payments in connection with an election
description: Whoever without the general or special authority in writing of a candidate incurs or authorises expenses on account of the holding of any public meeting, or upon any advertisement, circular or publication, or in any other way whatsoever for the purpose of promoting or procuring the election of such candidate, shall be punished with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees: Provided that if any person having incurred any such expenses not exceeding the amount of ten rupees without authority obtains within ten days from the date on which such expenses were incurred the approval in writing of the candidate, he shall be deemed to have incurred such expenses with the authority of the candidate.
summary: Incurring or authorising election‑related expenses without a candidate’s written authority is punishable by a fine up to ten thousand rupees, subject to a limited exemption for expenses not exceeding ten rupees if later approved in writing.
punishment: fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees
ingredients: incurs or authorises expenses for public meeting, advertisement, circular, publication or any other means
purpose is to promote or procure the election of a candidate
does so without general or special authority in writing of the candidate
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 176, Page 52

title: Failure to keep election accounts
description: Whoever being required by any law for the time being in force or any rule having the force of law to keep accounts of expenses incurred at or in connection with an election fails to keep such accounts shall be punished with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees.
summary: Any person legally required to maintain election expense accounts who fails to do so is liable to a fine up to five thousand rupees.
punishment: fine which may extend to five thousand rupees
ingredients: is required by law or rule to keep accounts of election expenses
fails to keep such accounts
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 177, Page 52

title: Counterfeiting coin, Government stamps, currency‑notes or bank‑notes
description: Whoever counterfeits, or knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting, any coin, stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, currency-note or bank-note, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: Counterfeiting any government‑issued coin, stamp, currency‑note or bank‑note, or participating in the process, is punishable by life imprisonment, or up to ten years and a fine.
punishment: imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine
ingredients: counterfeits any coin, stamp, currency‑note or bank‑note issued by Government for revenue purposes
knowingly performs any part of the process of counterfeiting
explanation: For the purposes of this Chapter,— (1) the expression “bank-note” means a promissory note or engagement for the payment of money to bearer on demand issued by any person carrying on the business of banking in any part of the world, or issued by or under the authority of any State or Sovereign Power, and intended to be used as equivalent to, or as a substitute for money; (2) “coin” shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in section 2 of the Coinage Act, 2011 and includes metal used for the time being as money and is stamped and issued by or under the authority of any State or Sovereign Power intended to be so used; (3) a person commits the offence of “counterfeiting Government stamp” who counterfeits by causing a genuine stamp of one denomination to appear like a genuine stamp of a different denomination; (4) a person commits the offence of counterfeiting coin who intending to practise deception, or knowing it to be likely that deception will thereby be practised, causes a genuine coin to appear like a different coin; and (5) the offence of “counterfeiting coin” includes diminishing the weight or alteration of the composition, or alteration of the appearance of the coin.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 178, Page 52

title: Importing, exporting, selling, delivering, buying, receiving, trafficking or using as genuine forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency‑note or bank‑note
description: Whoever imports or exports, or sells or delivers to, or buys or receives from, any other person, or otherwise traffics or uses as genuine, any forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency-note or bank-note, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be forged or counterfeit, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: Importing, exporting, selling, delivering, buying, receiving, trafficking or using as genuine any forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency‑note or bank‑note, with knowledge of its falsity, attracts life imprisonment, or up to ten years and a fine.
punishment: imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine
ingredients: imports, exports, sells, delivers, buys, receives, traffics or uses as genuine any forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency‑note or bank‑note
knows or has reason to believe the item is forged or counterfeit
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 179, Page 52

title: Possession of forged or counterfeit coin, Government stamp, currency‑notes or bank‑notes
description: Whoever has in his possession any forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency-note or bank-note, knowing or having reason to believe the same to be forged or counterfeit and intending to use the same as genuine or that it may be used as genuine, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both.
summary: Possessing forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency‑note or bank‑note with knowledge of its falsity and intent to use it as genuine is punishable by up to seven years imprisonment, a fine, or both.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: has possession of forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency‑note or bank-note
knows or has reason to believe it is forged or counterfeit
intends to use it as genuine or that it may be used as genuine
explanation: If a person establishes the possession of the forged or counterfeit coin, stamp, currency-note or bank-note to be from a lawful source, it shall not constitute an offence under this section.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 180, Page 52

description: Whoever makes or mends, or performs any part of the process of making or mending, or buys or sells or disposes of, or has in his possession, any machinery, die, or instrument or material for the purpose of being used, or knowing or having reason to believe that it is intended to be used, for forging or counterfeiting any coin, stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, currency-note or bank-note, shall be punished with imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: Makes, possesses, or deals in equipment intended for forging or counterfeiting coins, stamps, currency notes or bank notes and is punishable with life imprisonment or up to ten years and a fine.
punishment: imprisonment for life, or with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to ten years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ingredients: makes, mends or performs any part of making or mending machinery, die, instrument or material
buys, sells, disposes of or possesses such equipment
knowledge or reason to believe it will be used for forging or counterfeiting
targeted objects: coin, government stamp, currency‑note or bank‑note
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 181, Page 53

description: ( 1 ) Whoever makes, or causes to be made, or uses for any purpose whatsoever, or delivers to any person, any document purporting to be, or in any way resembling, or so nearly resembling as to be calculated to deceive, any currency-note or bank-note shall be punished with fine which may extend to three hundred rupees. ( 2 ) If any person, whose name appears on a document the making of which is an offence under sub-section ( 1 ), refuses, without lawful excuse, to disclose to a police officer on being so required the name and address of the person by whom it was printed or otherwise made, he shall be punished with fine which may extend to six hundred rupees. ( 3 ) Where the name of any person appears on any document in respect of which any person is charged with an offence under sub-section ( 1 ) or on any other document used or distributed in connection with that document it may, until the contrary is proved, be presumed that the person caused the document to be made.
summary: Penalises making, causing to be made, using, or delivering documents that resemble currency or bank notes, with fines up to Rs.300; also fines up to Rs.600 for refusing to disclose the printer’s details, and creates a presumption of causation when a name appears on such documents.
punishment: fine which may extend to three hundred rupees; fine which may extend to six hundred rupees
ingredients: makes, causes to be made, uses, or delivers a document resembling a currency‑note or bank‑note
document is calculated to deceive
refusal to disclose printer’s name and address when required by police
presumption that a named person caused the document to be made
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 182, Page 53

description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to the Government, removes or effaces from any substance, bearing any stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, any writing or document for which such stamp has been used, or removes from any writing or document a stamp which has been used for such writing or document, in order that such stamp may be used for a different writing or document, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
summary: Punishes fraudulent removal or effacement of revenue stamps from substances or documents with the intent to reuse the stamp, carrying up to three years imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: removes or effaces a revenue stamp from any substance or document
fraudulent intent or intent to cause loss to the Government
purpose is to reuse the stamp for a different writing or document
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 183, Page 53

description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to the Government, uses for any purpose a stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, which he knows to have been before used, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
summary: Penalises the use of a previously used revenue stamp for any purpose with fraudulent intent, punishable by up to two years imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: uses a revenue stamp that has been previously used
knows the stamp has been previously used
fraudulent intent or intent to cause loss to the Government
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 184, Page 53

description: Whoever, fraudulently or with intent to cause loss to Government, erases or removes from a stamp issued by Government for the purpose of revenue, any mark, put or impressed upon such stamp for the purpose of denoting that the same has been used, or knowingly has in his possession or sells or disposes of any such stamp from which such mark has been erased or removed, or sells or disposes of any such stamp which he knows to have been used, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
summary: Makes it an offence to erase or remove the usage mark from a revenue stamp, or to possess, sell or dispose of such a stamp knowing the mark has been removed, punishable by up to three years imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: erases or removes the usage mark from a revenue stamp
knows the stamp has been used
possesses, sells or disposes of the stamp after mark removal
fraudulent intent or intent to cause loss to Government
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 185, Page 53

summary: Criminalises the making, dealing in, possessing, or using fictitious postage stamps, as well as possessing the tools to make them, with a fine up to Rs.200; also provides for seizure and forfeiture of such items and defines "fictitious stamp".
punishment: fine which may extend to two hundred rupees
ingredients: makes, knowingly utters, deals in or sells any fictitious stamp
uses a fictitious stamp for any postal purpose
possesses a fictitious stamp without lawful excuse
makes or possesses any die, plate, instrument or material for making a fictitious stamp
possession of such items may be seized and forfeited
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 186, Page 53

description: Whoever, being employed in any mint lawfully established in India, does any act, or omits what he is legally bound to do, with the intention of causing any coin issued from that mint to be of a different weight or composition from the weight or composition fixed by law, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: Penalises mint employees who intentionally alter the weight or composition of coins produced, punishable by up to seven years imprisonment and a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine
ingredients: employed in a mint lawfully established in India
does an act or omission contrary to legal duty
intention to cause a coin to have different weight or composition than prescribed by law
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 187, Page 54

description: Whoever, without lawful authority, takes out of any mint, lawfully established in India, any coining tool or instrument, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: Makes it an offence for any person to take a coining tool or instrument from a legally established mint without authority, punishable by up to seven years imprisonment and a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to seven years, and shall also be liable to fine
ingredients: takes out any coining tool or instrument
from a mint lawfully established in India
without lawful authority
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 188, Page 54

description: ( 1 )An assembly of five or more persons is designated an “unlawful assembly”, if the common object of the persons composing that assembly is — ( a ) tooverawebycriminalforce,orshowofcriminalforce,theCentralGovernment or any State Government or Parliament or the Legislature of any State, or any public servant in the exercise of the lawful power of such public servant; or ( b ) to resist the execution of any law, or of any legal process; or ( c ) to commit any mischief or criminal trespass, or other offence; or ( d )   by means of criminal force, or show of criminal force, to any person, to take or obtain possession of any property, or to deprive any person of the enjoyment of a right of way, or of the use of water or other incorporeal right of which he is in possession or enjoyment, or to enforce any right or supposed right; or ( e )   by means of criminal force, or show of criminal force, to compel any person to do what he is not legally bound to do, or to omit to do what he is entitled to do. Explanation . — An assembly which was not unlawful when it assembled, may subsequently become an unlawful assembly. ( 2 ) Whoever, being aware of facts which render any assembly an unlawful assembly, intentionally joins that assembly, or continues in it, is said to be a member of an unlawful assembly and such member shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both. ( 3 ) Whoever joins or continues in an unlawful assembly, knowing that such unlawful assembly has been commanded in the manner prescribed by law to disperse, shall be punished with imprison
summary: Defines an unlawful assembly of five or more persons with specific common objects, provides that members may be punished with up to six months imprisonment or fine, and includes an explanation that an assembly can become unlawful after forming.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: assembly of five or more persons
common object includes any of the listed items (a) to (e)
knowledge of facts rendering the assembly unlawful
intentionally joining or continuing in the assembly
explanation: An assembly which was not unlawful when it assembled, may subsequently become an unlawful assembly.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 189, Page 54

description: 190.  If an offence is committed by any member of an unlawful assembly in prosecution of the common object of that assembly, or such as the members of that assembly knew to be likely to be committed in prosecution of that object, every person who, at the time of the committing of that offence, is a member of the same assembly, is guilty of that offence .
summary: Any person who is a member of an unlawful assembly at the time an offence is committed in prosecution of the assembly's common object, or an offence the members knew was likely to be committed, is deemed guilty of that offence.
ingredients: member of unlawful assembly at the time of the offence
offence committed in prosecution of the common object of the assembly or an offence the members knew was likely to be committed
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 190, Page 55

description: 191.  ( 1 ) Whenever force or violence is used by an unlawful assembly, or by any member thereof, in prosecution of the common object of such assembly, every member of such assembly is guilty of the offence of rioting. ( 2 ) Whoever is guilty of rioting, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. ( 3 ) Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both.
summary: All members of an unlawful assembly that uses force or violence to pursue its common object are guilty of rioting. The punishment is up to two years' imprisonment, or up to five years if armed with a deadly weapon.
punishment: Whoever is guilty of rioting, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.; Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with anything which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to five years, or with fine, or with both.
ingredients: use of force or violence by an unlawful assembly in prosecution of its common object
participation as a member of such assembly
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 191, Page 55

description: 192.  Whoever malignantly, or wantonly by doing anything which is illegal, gives provocation to any person intending or knowing it to be likely that such provocation will cause the offence of rioting to be committed, shall, if the offence of rioting be committed in consequence of such provocation, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both; and if the offence of rioting be not committed, with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
summary: A person who maliciously or wantonly provokes another, knowing it is likely to cause rioting, is punished if rioting occurs (up to one year) or if it does not occur (up to six months).
punishment: shall, if the offence of rioting be committed in consequence of such provocation, be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both; and if the offence of rioting be not committed, with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine, or with both.
ingredients: malignant or wanton illegal act giving provocation
knowledge that provocation is likely to cause rioting
whether rioting actually occurs or not
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 192, Page 55

description: 193.  ( 1 ) Whenever any unlawful assembly or riot takes place, the owner or occupier of the land upon which such unlawful assembly is held, or such riot is committed, and any person having or claiming an interest in such land, shall be punishable with fine not exceeding one thousand rupees, if he or his agent or manager, knowing that such offence is being or has been committed, or having reason to believe it is likely to be committed, do not give the earliest notice thereof in his or their power to the officer in charge at the nearest police station, and do not, in the case of his or their having reason to believe that it was about to be committed, use all lawful means in his or their power to prevent it and, in the event of its taking place, do not use all lawful means in his or their power to disperse or suppress the riot or unlawful assembly. ( 2 ) Whenever a riot is committed for the benefit or on behalf of any person who is the owner or occupier of any land respecting which such riot takes place or who claims any interest in such land, or in the subject of any dispute which gave rise to the riot, or who has accepted or derived any benefit therefrom, such person shall be punishable with fine, if he or his agent or manager, having reason to believe that such riot was likely to be committed or that the unlawful assembly by which such riot was committed was likely to be held, shall not respectively use all lawful means in his or their power to prevent such assembly or riot from taking place, and for suppressing and dispersing the same.
summary: Owners, occupiers, or interested persons of land where an unlawful assembly or riot occurs are liable to a fine if they fail to notify police or to use lawful means to prevent or disperse the disturbance, especially when they have reason to believe it will happen.
punishment: shall be punishable with fine not exceeding one thousand rupees; shall be punishable with fine
ingredients: owner, occupier, or person claiming interest in land where assembly/riot occurs
knowledge or reason to believe the offence is being or likely to be committed
failure to give notice to police
failure to use lawful means to prevent or disperse the assembly/riot
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 193, Page 56

title: Affray
description: 194.  ( 1 ) When two or more persons, by fighting in a public place, disturb the public peace, they are said to commit an affray. ( 2 ) Whoever commits an affray, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: An affray occurs when two or more persons fight in a public place and disturb public peace. The offender is liable to imprisonment up to one month, a fine up to Rs.1,000, or both.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both
ingredients: two or more persons
by fighting in a public place
disturb the public peace
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 194, Page 56

title: Assaulting or obstructing public servant when suppressing riot, etc.
description: 195.  ( 1 ) Whoever assaults or obstructs any public servant or uses criminal force on any public servant in the discharge of his duty as such public servant in endeavouring to disperse an unlawful assembly, or to suppress a riot or affray, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine which shall not be less than twenty-five thousand rupees, or with both. ( 2 ) Whoever threatens to assault or attempts to obstruct any public servant or threatens or attempts to use criminal force to any public servant in the discharge of his duty as such public servant in endeavouring to disperse an unlawful assembly, or to suppress a riot or affray, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
summary: Any person who assaults, obstructs, or uses criminal force against a public servant while the servant is dispersing an unlawful assembly, suppressing a riot or affray, faces up to three years’ imprisonment and a minimum fine of Rs.25,000. Threats or attempts without actual force attract up to one year’s imprisonment or fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine which shall not be less than twenty-five thousand rupees, or with both; imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: assaults or obstructs any public servant
uses criminal force on any public servant
public servant is discharging duty to disperse unlawful assembly, suppress riot or affray
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 195, Page 56

title: Promoting enmity between different groups on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, etc., and doing acts prejudicial to maintenance of harmony
description: 196.  ( 1 ) Whoever— ( a ) by words, either spoken or written, or by signs or by visible representations or through electronic communication or otherwise, promotes or attempts to promote, on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, caste or community or any other ground whatsoever, disharmony or feelings of enmity, hatred or ill-will between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes or communities; or ( b ) commits any act which is prejudicial to the maintenance of harmony between different religious, racial, language or regional groups or castes or communities, and which disturbs or is likely to disturb the public tranquillity; or ( c ) organises any exercise, movement, drill or other similar activity intending that the participants in such activity shall use or be trained to use criminal force or violence or knowing it to be likely that the participants in such activity will use or be trained to use criminal force or violence, or participates in such activity intending to use or be trained to use criminal force or violence or knowing it to be likely that the participants in such activity will use or be trained to use criminal force or violence, against any religious, racial, language or regional group or caste or community and such activity for any reason whatsoever causes or is likely to cause fear or alarm or a feeling of insecurity amongst members of such religious, racial, language or regional group or caste or community, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. ( 2 ) Whoever commits an offence specified in sub-section ( 1 ) in any place of worship or in any assembly engaged in the performance of religious worship or religious ceremonies, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to five years and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: The section criminalises acts that promote enmity or disharmony between religious, racial, linguistic or regional groups, including prejudicial acts and organizing violent activities against such groups. If the offence is committed in a place of worship, the maximum imprisonment increases to five years.
punishment: imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both; imprisonment which may extend to five years and shall also be liable to fine
ingredients: promotes or attempts to promote disharmony or enmity between different groups on grounds of religion, race, place of birth, residence, language, caste or community or any other ground
commits any act prejudicial to maintenance of harmony between such groups that disturbs or is likely to disturb public tranquillity
organises or participates in activity intending or likely to use criminal force or violence against any such group, causing fear, alarm or insecurity among its members
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 196, Page 56

title: Imputations, assertions prejudicial to national integration
description: 197.  ( 1 ) Whoever, by words either spoken or written or by signs or by visible representations or through electronic communication or otherwise,— ( a ) makes or publishes any imputation that any class of persons cannot, by reason of their being members of any religious, racial, language or regional group or caste or community, bear true faith and allegiance to the Constitution of India as by law established or uphold the sovereignty and integrity of India; or ( b ) asserts, counsels, advises, propagates or publishes that any class of persons shall, by reason of their being members of any religious, racial, language or regional group or caste or community, be denied, or deprived of their rights as citizens of India; or ( c ) makes or publishes any assertion, counsel, plea or appeal concerning the obligation of any class of persons, by reason of their being members of any religious, racial, language or regional group or caste or community, and such assertion, counsel, plea or appeal causes or is likely to cause disharmony or feelings of enmity or hatred or ill-will between such members and other persons; or ( d ) makes or publishes false or misleading information, jeopardising the sovereignty, unity and integrity or security of India, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both. ( 2 ) Whoever commits an offence specified in sub-section ( 1 ) in any place of worship or in any assembly engaged in the performance of religious worship or religious ceremonies, shall be punished with imprisonment which may extend to five years and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: The section penalises publishing or circulating statements that question the loyalty, rights, or obligations of any community, or that spread false information endangering India's sovereignty. Offences committed in places of worship attract a higher maximum imprisonment of five years.
punishment: imprisonment which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both; imprisonment which may extend to five years and shall also be liable to fine
ingredients: makes or publishes any imputation that a class of persons cannot bear true faith and allegiance to the Constitution or uphold sovereignty of India
asserts, counsels, advises, propagates or publishes that a class of persons shall be denied or deprived of their rights as citizens
makes or publishes any assertion, counsel, plea or appeal concerning obligations of a class of persons that is likely to cause disharmony, enmity, hatred or ill‑will
makes or publishes false or misleading information jeopardising sovereignty, unity, integrity or security of India
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 197, Page 57

title: Public servant disobeying law, with intent to cause injury to any person
description: 198.  Whoever, being a public servant, knowingly disobeys any direction of the law as to the way in which he is to conduct himself as such public servant, intending to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will by such disobedience, cause injury to any person, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
summary: A public servant who deliberately ignores a legal direction with the intention or knowledge that it may cause injury to any person is liable to simple imprisonment up to one year, a fine, or both.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both
ingredients: public servant
knowingly disobeys any direction of law as to conduct
intending to cause or knowing it is likely to cause injury to any person
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 198, Page 57

title: Public servant disobeying direction of law relating to investigation
description: 199.  Whoever, being a public servant,— ( a )knowinglydisobeysanydirectionofthelawwhichprohibitshim from requiring the attendance at any place of any person for the purpose of investigation into an offence or any other matter; or ( b ) knowingly disobeys, to the prejudice of any person, any other direction of the law regulating the manner in which he shall conduct such investigation;
summary: A public servant who knowingly violates a legal direction that prohibits demanding a person's attendance for investigation, or who knowingly breaches any other investigative direction to the prejudice of a person, commits an offence.
ingredients: public servant
knowingly disobeys any direction of law prohibiting him from requiring attendance of any person for investigation
knowingly disobeys, to the prejudice of any person, any other direction of law regulating the manner of investigation
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 199, Page 58

description: 200. Whoever, being in charge of a hospital, public or private, whether run by the Central Government, the State Government, local bodies or any other person, contravenes the provisions of section 397 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
summary: A person in charge of any hospital who violates the provisions of section 397 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita commits an offence.
punishment: imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both.
ingredients: being in charge of a hospital (public or private)
contravention of the provisions of section 397 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 200, Page 58

description: 201. Whoever, being a public servant, and being, as such public servant, charged with the preparation or translation of any document or electronic record, frames, prepares or translates that document or electronic record in a manner which he knows or believes to be incorrect, intending thereby to cause or knowing it to be likely that he may thereby cause injury to any person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
summary: A public servant who, while tasked with preparing or translating a document or electronic record, knowingly makes it incorrect with intent or knowledge that it may cause injury, commits an offence.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both.
ingredients: being a public servant
charged with preparation or translation of a document or electronic record
prepares, frames or translates the document/record in a manner known or believed to be incorrect
intention to cause injury or knowledge that injury is likely
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 201, Page 58

description: 202. Whoever, being a public servant, and being legally bound as such public servant not to engage in trade, engages in trade, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both or with community service.
summary: A public servant who is legally prohibited from engaging in trade but does so commits an offence.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine, or with both or with community service.
ingredients: being a public servant
legally bound not to engage in trade
engages in trade
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 202, Page 58

description: 203. Whoever, being a public servant, and being legally bound as such public servant, not to purchase or bid for certain property, purchases or bids for that property, either in his own name or in the name of another, or jointly, or in shares with others, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and the property, if purchased, shall be confiscated.
summary: A public servant who is legally barred from purchasing or bidding for specified property and does so, in any manner, commits an offence and the property may be confiscated.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both; and the property, if purchased, shall be confiscated.
ingredients: being a public servant
legally bound not to purchase or bid for certain property
purchases or bids for that property (in own name, another's name, jointly, or in shares)
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 203, Page 58

description: 204. Whoever pretends to hold any particular office as a public servant, knowing that he does not hold such office or falsely personates any other person holding such office, and in such assumed character does or attempts to do any act under colour of such office, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to three years and with fine.
summary: A person who falsely claims to be a public servant and acts or attempts to act under that false pretence commits an offence.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which shall not be less than six months but which may extend to three years and with fine.
ingredients: pretends to hold a public servant office
knows that he does not hold such office or falsely personates another
does or attempts to do any act under the colour of that office
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 204

description: 205. Whoever, not belonging to a certain class of public servants, wears any garb or carries any token resembling any garb or token used by that class of public servants, with the intention that it may be believed, or with the knowledge that it is likely to be believed, that he belongs to that class of public servants, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: A person not a public servant who wears or carries garb or tokens resembling those of a public servant class, intending or knowing it will be believed he belongs to that class, commits an offence.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: not belonging to a certain class of public servants
wears or carries garb or token resembling that class
intention or knowledge that it will be believed he belongs to that class
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 205, Page 58

description: 206. Whoever absconds in order to avoid being served with a summons, notice or order proceeding from any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to issue such summons, notice or order,–– ( a ) shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; ( b )   where such summons or notice or order is to attend in person or by agent, or to produce a document or an electronic record in a Court shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: A person who absconds to avoid service of a summons, notice or order from a competent public servant commits an offence, with higher punishment if the document requires personal appearance in court.
punishment: (a) simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; (b) simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: absconds to avoid being served with a summons, notice or order
the summons, notice or order is issued by a public servant legally competent to issue it
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 206, Page 59

description: 207. Whoever in any manner intentionally prevents the serving on himself, or on any other person, of any summons, notice or order proceeding from any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to issue such summons, notice or order, or intentionally prevents the lawful affixing to any place of any such summons, notice or order or intentionally removes any such summons, notice or order from any place to which it is lawfully affixed or intentionally prevents the lawful making of any proclamation, under the authority of any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to direct such proclamation to be made,–– ( a )   shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; ( b )   where the summons, notice, order or proclamation is to attend in person or by agent, or to produce a document or electronic record in a Court, with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: A person who intentionally obstructs service, affixing, removal, or proclamation of a summons, notice, order or proclamation issued by a competent public servant commits an offence, with higher punishment when personal appearance in court is involved.
punishment: (a) simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; (b) simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: intentionally prevents service of a summons, notice or order on self or another
intentionally prevents lawful affixing of such summons, notice or order
intentionally removes such summons, notice or order from a lawfully affixed place
intentionally prevents lawful making of any proclamation under authority of a competent public servant
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 207, Page 59

description: 208. Whoever, being legally bound to attend in person or by an agent at a certain place and time in obedience to a summons, notice, order, or proclamation proceeding from any public servant legally competent, as such public servant, to issue the same, intentionally omits to attend at that place or time or departs from the place where he is bound to attend before the time at which it is lawful for him to depart,–– ( a )   shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; ( b )   where the summons, notice, order or proclamation is to attend in person or by agent in a Court with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both. Illustrations. ( a ) A, being legally bound to appear before a High Court, in obedience to a subpoena issuing from that Court, intentionally omits to appear. A has committed the offence defined in this section. ( b ) A, being legally bound to appear before a District Judge, as a witness, in obedience to a summons issued by that District Judge intentionally omits to appear.A has committed the offence defined in this section.
summary: A person legally bound to appear before a public servant's summons, notice, order or proclamation who intentionally fails to appear or departs early commits an offence; higher punishment applies when the appearance is required in a court.
punishment: (a) simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; (b) simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: legally bound to attend in person or by agent at a specified place and time in obedience to a summons, notice, order or proclamation
intentionally omits to attend or departs before the lawful time of departure
explanation: Illustrations. ( a ) A, being legally bound to appear before a High Court, in obedience to a subpoena issuing from that Court, intentionally omits to appear. A has committed the offence defined in this section. ( b ) A, being legally bound to appear before a District Judge, as a witness, in obedience to a summons issued by that District Judge intentionally omits to appear.A has committed the offence defined in this section.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 208, Page 59

description: 209. Whoever fails to appear at the specified place and the specified time as required by a proclamation published under sub-section ( 1 ) of section 84 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023, shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both, or with community service, and where a declaration has been made under sub-section ( 4 ) of that section pronouncing him as a proclaimed offender, he shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: A person who does not appear as required by a proclamation under section 84 commits an offence, with a higher penalty if declared a proclaimed offender.
punishment: imprisonment for a term which may extend to three years, or with fine, or with both, or with community service, and where a declaration has been made under sub-section (4) of that section pronouncing him as a proclaimed offender, he shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may extend to seven years and shall also be liable to fine.
ingredients: fails to appear at the specified place and time as required by a proclamation under sub-section (1) of section 84 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023
if a declaration under sub-section (4) of that section pronounces the person as a proclaimed offender
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 209, Page 59

title: Omission to produce document or electronic record to public servant by person legally bound to produce it
description: 210.  Whoever, being legally bound to produce or deliver up any document or electronic record to any public servant, as such, intentionally omits so to produce or deliver up the same,–– ( a ) shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; ( b ) and where the document or electronic record is to be produced or delivered up to a Court with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both. Illustration. A, being legally bound to produce a document before a District Court, intentionally omits to produce the same. A has committed the offence defined in this section.
summary: A person legally bound to produce or deliver a document or electronic record who intentionally fails to do so is punishable. The punishment is up to one month imprisonment (or fine) for general public servants, and up to six months if the document is to be produced before a court.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; and where the document or electronic record is to be produced or delivered up to a Court, simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: legally bound to produce or deliver a document or electronic record to a public servant
intentionally omitted to produce or deliver the document or record
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 210, Page 60

title: Omission to give notice or information to public servant by person legally bound to give it
description: 211.  Whoever, being legally bound to give any notice or to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, as such, intentionally omits to give such notice or to furnish such information in the manner and at the time required by law,–– ( a ) shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; ( b )   where the notice or information required to be given respects the commission of an offence, or is required for the purpose of preventing the commission of an offence, or in order to the apprehension of an offender, with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both; ( c ) where the notice or information required to be given is required by an order passed under section 394 of the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: A person legally bound to give notice or information who intentionally fails to do so is punishable. The punishment varies: up to one month imprisonment for ordinary omissions, up to six months if the information relates to an offence, and up to six months (or fine) if required by a specific order under section 394.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; where the notice or information respects the commission of an offence, simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both; where the notice or information is required by an order under section 394, imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to one thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: legally bound to give notice or furnish information to a public servant
intentionally omitted to give the notice or information in the manner and time required by law
explanation: In section 211 and in this section the word “offence” include any act committed at any place out of India, which, if committed in India, would be punishable under any of the following sections, namely, 103, 105, 307, sub-sections ( 2 ), ( 3 ) and ( 4 ) of section 309, sub-sections ( 2 ), ( 3 ), ( 4 ) and ( 5 ) of section 310, 311, 312, clauses ( f ) and ( g ) of section 326, sub-sections ( 4 ), ( 6 ), ( 7 ) and ( 8 ) of section 331, clauses ( a ) and ( b ) of section 332 and the word “offender” includes any person who is alleged to have been guilty of any such act.
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 211, Page 60

title: Furnishing false information
description: 212.  Whoever, being legally bound to furnish information on any subject to any public servant, as such, furnishes, as true, information on the subject which he knows or has reason to believe to be false,–– ( a ) shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; ( b ) where the information which he is legally bound to give respects the commission of an offence, or is required for the purpose of preventing the commission of an offence, or in order to the apprehension of an offender, with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both. Illustrations. ( a )A, a landholder, knowing of the commission of a murder within the limits of his estate, wilfully misinforms the Magistrate of the district that the death has occurred by accident in consequence of the bite of a snake. Ais guilty of the offence defined in this section. ( b ) A, a village watchman, knowing that a considerable body of strangers has passed through his village in order to commit a dacoity in the house of Z, a wealthy merchant residing in a neighbouring place, and being legally bound to give early and punctual information of the above fact to the officer of the nearest police station, wilfully misinforms the police officer that a body of suspicious characters passed through the village with a view to commit dacoity in a certain distant place in a different direction. Here A is guilty of the offence defined in this section.
summary: A person legally bound to furnish information who deliberately provides false information is punishable. The punishment is up to six months imprisonment (or fine) for general false information, and up to two years imprisonment (or fine) if the false information relates to an offence.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both; where the information respects the commission of an offence, imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or with both.
ingredients: legally bound to furnish information on any subject to a public servant
furnishes information as true which he knows or has reason to believe is false
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 212, Page 60

title: Refusal to bind oneself by oath or affirmation to state truth when required by public servant
description: 213.  Whoever refuses to bind himself by an oath or affirmation to state the truth, when required so to bind himself by a public servant legally competent to require that he shall so bind himself, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: Anyone who refuses to take an oath or affirmation to tell the truth when a competent public servant requires it is punishable with up to six months imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: required by a public servant to bind himself by oath or affirmation to state truth
refuses to bind himself
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 213, Page 61

title: Refusal to answer question when legally bound to state truth
description: 214.  Whoever, being legally bound to state the truth on any subject to any public servant, refuses to answer any question demanded of him touching that subject by such public servant in the exercise of the legal powers of such public servant, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: A person who is legally bound to tell the truth to a public servant and refuses to answer a question on that subject is punishable with up to six months imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: legally bound to state the truth on any subject to a public servant
refuses to answer any question demanded by that public servant
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 214, Page 61

title: Refusal to sign statement when required
description: 215.  Whoever refuses to sign any statement made by him, when required to sign that statement by a public servant legally competent to require that he shall sign that statement, shall be punished with simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to three thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: Anyone who refuses to sign a statement when a competent public servant lawfully requires it is punishable with up to three months imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: simple imprisonment for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to three thousand rupees, or with both.
ingredients: required by a public servant to sign a statement
refuses to sign the statement
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 215, Page 61

title: Making false statement while bound by oath or affirmation
description: 216.  Whoever, being legally bound by an oath or affirmation to state the truth on any subject to any public servant or other person authorised by law to administer such oath or affirmation, makes, to such public servant or other person as aforesaid, touching that subject, any statement which is false, and which he either knows or believes to be false or does not believe to be true, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.
summary: A person who, under oath or affirmation, knowingly makes a false statement to a public servant or authorised person is punishable with imprisonment up to three years and a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three years, and shall also be liable to fine.
ingredients: legally bound by oath or affirmation to state truth on any subject to a public servant or authorised person
makes a false statement knowing or believing it false or not believing it true
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 216, Page 61

title: Giving false information to public servant with intent to cause misuse of power
description: 217.  Whoever gives to any public servant any information which he knows or believes to be false, intending thereby to cause, or knowing it to be likely that he will thereby cause, such public servant— ( a ) to do or omit anything which such public servant ought not to do or omit if the true state of facts respecting which such information is given were known by him; or ( b ) to use the lawful power of such public servant to the injury or annoyance of any person, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which m
summary: A person who knowingly provides false information to a public servant with the intention or knowledge that it will cause the official to act improperly or misuse power is punishable, though the exact punishment term is incomplete in the source.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which m
ingredients: gives false information to a public servant
knows or believes the information is false
intends or knows it is likely to cause the public servant to do or omit something improper or to misuse power
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 217, Page 61

description: Whoever offers any resistance to the taking of any property by the lawful authority of any public servant, knowing or having reason to believe that he is such public servant, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: Any person who resists a public servant lawfully taking property, with knowledge of the servant's authority, commits an offence punishable by up to six months imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to six months, or with fine which may extend to ten thousand rupees, or with both
ingredients: offers any resistance to the taking of any property
by the lawful authority of any public servant
knowing or having reason to believe that he is such public servant
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 218, Page 62

description: Whoever intentionally obstructs any sale of property offered for sale by the lawful authority of any public servant, as such, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both.
summary: Intentionally obstructing a sale of property that is being offered by a public servant's lawful authority is an offence punishable by up to one month imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to five thousand rupees, or with both
ingredients: intentionally obstructs any sale of property
property offered for sale by the lawful authority of any public servant
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 219, Page 62

description: Whoever, at any sale of property held by the lawful authority of a public servant, as such, purchases or bids for any property on account of any person, whether himself or any other, whom he knows to be under a legal incapacity to purchase that property at that sale, or bids for such property not intending to perform the obligations under which he lays himself by such bidding, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to two hundred rupees, or with both.
summary: Purchasing or bidding for property at a public sale on behalf of someone legally incapable, or bidding without intention to fulfill obligations, is punishable by up to one month imprisonment or a fine.
punishment: imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to one month, or with fine which may extend to two hundred rupees, or with both
ingredients: at any sale of property held by the lawful authority of a public servant
purchases or bids for any property on account of any person
knows the person is under a legal incapacity to purchase
or bids without intending to perform the obligations arising from the bid
sourceReference: Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 (No. 45 of 2023), Gazette of India Extraordinary Part II Sec. 1, 25th December 2023, Section 220, Page 62

description: Whoever voluntarily obstructs any public servant in the discharge of his public functions, shall be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may extend to three months, or with fine which may extend to two thousand and five hundred rupees, or with both.
summary: Voluntarily obstructing a public servant while they perform official duties is an offence punishable by up to three months imprisonment or a fine.
